elements.text: Primary efficacy endpoint: Tobit regression results of AUC DENV-3 RNA viral load measured from Day 1 (baseline, immediately before inoculation) to day 29 (log10 copies/ml/28 days)
One participant not included in analysis since day 29 sample is missing and missing value could not be imputed according to SAP rules.

Tobit regression p-value 0.0001
Exact Wilcoxon rank sum test p-value 0.0008

Group 2**
Placebo (n=2)
Low dose (40/10 mg) (n=6)
Middle dose (200/50 mg) (n=6)

*Group 1 was split into group 1a = sentinel group with n=4 (2:2) and group 1b = remaining high dose with n=1 (4:8)
** Group 2 – in progress, data to be available later in 2023.
Key results from the dengue prophylaxis Phase 2a human challenge study1

Significant viral load reduction for the high dose cohort in a stringent human challenge study using a recombinant DENV-3 strain 
JNJ-1802 overall safe and well tolerated
PK exposure showed low inter-subject variability
Results for mid and low dose cohort (group 2) expected later in 2023
Successfully de-risked start of Ph2 field study exploring antiviral efficacy of JNJ-1802 against natural infection with any of the 4 DENV serotypes
1Durbin et al. Abstract presented at the 6th Asia Dengue Summit 2023, 15th–16th June 2023, Bangkok, Thailand.
